text.title: wireframing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for wireframing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding color to wireframes — even "just one accent color" for the primary button.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any color redirects stakeholder feedback from structure to aesthetics. A single colored button generates 20 minutes of color discussion in a review that should be about layout.

failure-mode.description: Wireframing only the default state — no empty, error, or loading states documented.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Missing states are discovered during development; developers implement default-only behavior and edge cases become bugs. State coverage must be explicit in the wireframe deliverable.

failure-mode.description: No annotations — wireframe boxes without any behavior documentation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An unannotated wireframe is a Rorschach test. Every developer and stakeholder interprets element behavior differently; the disagreement surfaces in QA, not in review.

failure-mode.description: Jumping to a tool before sketching — opening Figma as the first step.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Design tools impose grids, component libraries, and frame structures that commit the designer to a layout direction before sufficient exploration. Paper sketches are faster and cheaper to discard.

failure-mode.description: Using Lorem Ipsum placeholder text instead of real content volume estimates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lorem Ipsum hides information density problems. A navigation that fits "Home / About / Products" breaks with "Enterprise Resource Planning Module Configuration".

failure-mode.description: Creating desktop wireframes only and noting "mobile TBD."
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mobile layouts require different hierarchy decisions than desktop — they cannot be derived from desktop wireframes without explicit design work. Mobile is a separate deliverable, not a footnote.
